sourceMovie.title: Lila Says
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Lila Says works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Perfume: The Story of a Murderer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lila Says clicked for you, Perfume: The Story of a Murderer (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lila Says?
faq.answer: Try Perfume: The Story of a Murderer, Gone Baby Gone, The Dreamers—each shares DNA with Lila Says while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lila Says?
faq.answer: Lila Says (2005) is best described as Crime, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lila Says worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
